+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",

+  "mission": {
+    "title": "lima: Set instance name while starting",
+    "description": "Set instance name while starting. Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current lima setup",
+        "description": "Verify your lima version and configuration:\n```bash\nlimactl --version\n```\nThis feature requires a working lima installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review lima configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant lima configuration:\nRunning `limactl start foo.yml` automatically sets the instance name to `foo`. It would be nice to be able to set a name different than the file name. Ex. running `limactl start foo.yml --name bar` would create an instance named `bar`."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Set instance name while starting",
+        "description": "> * I guess that is the same as what you have in mind too with NAME attribute/arg to start along with filename/template URL\n\nYes, this sounds pretty similar. My suggestion would be to have both `--from` and `--from-default` options. `--from` would require an argument (URL, filename),\n```yaml\n> limactl start bar --from examples/foo.yaml\n>\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"Set instance name while starting\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "> * I guess that is the same as what you have in mind too with NAME attribute/arg to start along with filename/template URL\n\nYes, this sounds pretty similar. My suggestion would be to have both `--from` and `--from-default` options.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "> limactl start bar --from examples/foo.yaml\n>"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
